memberdef.type: std::variant< std::monostate, ByteTag, ShortTag, IntTag, LongTag, FloatTag, DoubleTag, StringTag, ByteArrayTag, IntArrayTag, ListTag, CompoundTag >
memberdef.definition: using endstone::nbt::Tag::Storage = std::variant<std::monostate, ByteTag, ShortTag, IntTag, LongTag, FloatTag, DoubleTag, StringTag, ByteArrayTag, IntArrayTag, ListTag, CompoundTag>
memberdef.name: Storage

briefdescription.para: Get the type of payload currently held by this tag.

simplesect.para: the tag type, or Type::End if the tag is empty

briefdescription.para: Get the number of elements held by this tag.

detaileddescription.para: For a list, compound or array this is the element count; for a string it is the character count; scalar tags report 1 and an empty tag reports 0.

simplesect.para: the element count

briefdescription.para: Check whether this tag holds no elements.

simplesect.para: true if size() is 0

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag with the given key, treating this tag as a compound.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a CompoundTag, and a missing key is inserted with an empty value.

parameterdescription.para: the key to access

simplesect.para: a reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a compound

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag at the given index, treating this tag as a list.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a ListTag.

parameterdescription.para: the index to access

simplesect.para: a reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a list

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag with the given key, treating this tag as a compound, with bounds checking.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a CompoundTag.

parameterdescription.para: the key to access

simplesect.para: a reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a compound
if the key is not present

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag with the given key, treating this tag as a compound, with bounds checking.

parameterdescription.para: the key to access

simplesect.para: a const reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a compound
if the key is not present

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag at the given index, treating this tag as a list, with bounds checking.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a ListTag.

parameterdescription.para: the index to access

simplesect.para: a reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a list
if the index is out of range

briefdescription.para: Access the child tag at the given index, treating this tag as a list, with bounds checking.

parameterdescription.para: the index to access

simplesect.para: a const reference to the child tag

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a list
if the index is out of range

briefdescription.para: Check whether this tag is a compound containing the given key.

parameterdescription.para: the key to look up

simplesect.para: true if this tag is a compound and contains the key, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Construct a child tag in place at the end of this list.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a ListTag.

parameterdescription.para: the arguments forwarded to the element constructor

simplesect.para: a reference to this tag's underlying list

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a list

briefdescription.para: Construct an entry in place in this compound.

detaileddescription.para: An empty tag is promoted to a CompoundTag.

parameterdescription.para: the arguments forwarded to construct the key/value entry

simplesect.para: a pair of an iterator to the entry and a bool that is true if a new entry was inserted

parameterdescription.para: if this tag is not a compound

briefdescription.para: Get the payload as the given tag type.

parameterdescription.para: the tag type to retrieve

simplesect.para: a reference to the held value

parameterdescription.para: if this tag does not currently hold a T

briefdescription.para: Get the payload as the given tag type.

parameterdescription.para: the tag type to retrieve

simplesect.para: a const reference to the held value

parameterdescription.para: if this tag does not currently hold a T

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to the payload if this tag holds the given type.

parameterdescription.para: the tag type to retrieve

simplesect.para: a pointer to the held value, or nullptr if this tag does not hold a T

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to the payload if this tag holds the given type.

parameterdescription.para: the tag type to retrieve

simplesect.para: a const pointer to the held value, or nullptr if this tag does not hold a T

briefdescription.para: Apply a visitor to the payload currently held by this tag.

parameterdescription.para: the visitor type, callable with any of the tag payload types (including std::monostate)
the visitor to invoke

simplesect.para: whatever the visitor returns

briefdescription.para: Apply a visitor to the payload currently held by this tag.

parameterdescription.para: the visitor type, callable with any of the tag payload types (including std::monostate)
the visitor to invoke

simplesect.para: whatever the visitor returns

briefdescription.para: Serialize this tag to binary NBT with an empty root name.

parameterdescription.para: std::endian::little (Bedrock) or std::endian::big (Java).
If true, use Bedrock network varint encoding.

simplesect.para: Binary NBT data.
endstone::nbt::dump(), endstone::nbt::load()

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &name, std::endian byte_order=std::endian::little, bool network=false) const
memberdef.name: dump

briefdescription.para: Serialize this tag to binary NBT with a root name.

parameterdescription.para: The root tag name.
std::endian::little (Bedrock) or std::endian::big (Java).
If true, use Bedrock network varint encoding.

simplesect.para: Binary NBT data.

briefdescription.para: A single NBT tag that may hold any of the supported payload types.

detaileddescription.para: A Tag behaves as a tagged union over every NBT type (byte, short, int, long, float, double, string, byte array, int array, list and compound). A default-constructed Tag is empty (the End type). Indexing or inserting into an empty Tag promotes it to a ListTag or CompoundTag as appropriate.
